{{Infobox character
|nom=Pigeon
|apparition=Appears in book 1
|status=Unknown
}}

== Biography ==

The Pigeon is a large bird encountered in the woods. Upon meeting Alice, the Pigeon mistakes her for a serpent, beating her with its wings while screaming the accusation. When Alice protests that she is not a serpent, the Pigeon reiterates its charge in a more subdued tone. The Pigeon then expresses frustration and emotional distress regarding serpents, indicating that despite having attempted multiple locations—the roots of trees, banks, and hedges—it has been unable to achieve satisfactory results with them.

[[Category:Characters]]
[[Category:Personnages du Tome 1]]
